content:
- Finished the runtime-restart replica stall task as stale/already-fixed.
- Verified the scenario passed three targeted reruns and again in make test-long; all required gates passed and I updated the task markdown accordingly.
- Ran task_switch, committed all .ralph/task updates in 51b78e0, pushed master successfully, and the worktree should now be clean.

- Read current task summaries and done-task summaries after startup.
- Confirmed there are no active bug tasks in current_tasks.md; existing bug items are all already completed in current_tasks_done.md.
- Need to inspect blocker tags and choose the highest-priority unblocked failing task, then write its task path into .ralph/current_task.txt and stop immediately.

- current_tasks.md only showed task headers, so I opened the underlying task files next.
- Verifying whether any later HA coverage tasks are blocked, and whether story sequencing makes task 02 the right next pick after completed task 01.
- Once blockers are confirmed, I will write exactly one task path into .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- Verified there are no <blocked_by> tags in the active HA or operator-reset task files, so selection is among unblocked failing tasks only.
- All high-priority failing tasks belong to the HA quorum survival story; operator ergonomics tasks are lower priority and cannot outrank them.
- Comparing the high-priority HA task descriptions now to pick the most foundational next step rather than blindly using file order.

- Chose .ralph/tasks/story-ha-quorum-survival-under-real-failures/02-task-add-whole-node-kill-and-partial-recovery-ha-e2e.md as the next task.
- Reasoning: no active unblocked bug tasks exist; high-priority failing HA tasks outrank low-priority operator-reset tasks; task 02 is the most foundational because multiple later HA tasks explicitly reuse or build on the whole-node outage semantics it introduces.
- Wrote the selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt and should stop immediately now.

- Read the previous codex progress log and .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Current task is .ralph/tasks/story-ha-quorum-survival-under-real-failures/02-task-add-whole-node-kill-and-partial-recovery-ha-e2e.md.
- Reading the task file now to determine whether I must write the first full plan, skeptically revise an existing plan, or execute.

- Read previous progress log and located the redesign task at .ralph/tasks/story-managed-start-intent-architecture/task-redesign-ha-startup-bootstrap-and-rejoin-around-authoritative-dcs-reconciliation.md.
- Found the main HA surfaces to inspect: src/dcs/*, src/runtime/node.rs, src/ha/*, tests/ha/support/*, and the related HA quorum survival task files.
- Next I am reading the redesign task in detail, then I will compare its stated invariants against the pending HA e2e task files and current code behavior to find missing edge cases or ambiguities.

- Inspected startup/bootstrap/rejoin authority code in src/runtime/node.rs, src/dcs/state.rs, src/dcs/store.rs, src/dcs/worker.rs, src/dcs/etcd_store.rs, and src/ha/{decision,decide,process_dispatch,apply}. 
- Confirmed current startup planning treats DCS probe failure as optional for empty/missing data dirs: no cache means initialize primary, while existing PGDATA resumes as primary unless local managed replica residue exists, in which case startup errors. Init lock is the only durable cluster-initialized fact; leader is an etcd leased /scope/leader key, not a durable bootstrap fact.
- Next I am sending the concise report with line-numbered references, including current election/rejoin rules and redesign edge cases around stale member fallback, empty DCS after outage, and authority gaps between startup and runtime HA.

- Compared pending HA quorum-survival Ralph tasks against current e2e coverage in tests/ha_multi_node_failover.rs, tests/ha_partition_isolation.rs, tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs, tests/ha/support/partition.rs, and src/test_harness/ha_e2e/handle.rs.
- Confirmed current multi-node failover coverage still centers on postgres-stop and runtime-restart helpers, with no first-class full whole-node hard-kill scenario and no full 1:2 all-path partition helper yet.
- Next useful step would be to turn this mapping into redesign notes: reuse existing stop_node/restart_node plumbing, add explicit hard-kill/full-node assertions, and preserve current strong no-dual-primary + proof-write checks while adding bounded pre-heal quorum assertions.

- Reviewed the redesign task against the actual startup/DCS/HA code and the pending HA quorum-survival e2e task files.
- Confirmed the task is strong but not perfect: it misses scope references to tasks 03 and 04, leaves the durable expected-cluster identity under-specified, and should state the bootstrap ordering and offline descriptor publication rules more explicitly.
- Also mapped current e2e coverage: tasks 08 and parts of 06/07/10/11 already have partial or substantial coverage, while 02/03/04/05/09 remain materially missing. Next useful action would be to edit the task file to add the missing scope items, cluster identity record details, startup publication semantics, and the open questions I’m about to present to the user.

- User pushed back correctly on the interpretation risk: the task direction was already right, but some prohibitions and replacement requirements should be made more explicit so assignees cannot preserve the old startup authority shortcuts.
- I am editing the redesign task to harden the wording around: never becoming primary without fresh DCS quorum, deleting all foreign-healthy-primary fallback behavior, leader lease semantics when self-health is lost, and making the bootstrap-lock then cluster_initialized success ordering explicit.
- I will also add the missing related Ralph task references for network partition and storage stall coverage so the scope list is complete.

- User clarified another important missing requirement: the redesign/test scope should explicitly require a hard node-kill helper, not just pg stop or runtime restart.
- I am updating the redesign task so it requires HA e2e harness support for killing a node as a node-level failure (pg + api/runtime, optionally etcd where colocated) via hard OS kill semantics.
- This is meant to prevent future weak tests that only cover mellow shutdowns while claiming hard-failure coverage.

- Updated task 02 instead of the redesign task, per user correction.
- Tightened task 02 so “whole node failure” now requires a first-class helper for node-owned process-set kill semantics, at minimum runtime/API plus PostgreSQL, with optional colocated etcd where relevant.
- Also made the acceptance criteria explicitly reject weak implementations that manually chain soft stop helpers and call that hard node death coverage.
